raw_consumer_statement:
What happened? 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased a XXXXXXXX XXXX  from Best Buy for {$1600.00} using my Best Buy credit card. I later returned this TV and exchanged it for another model on XX/XX/XXXX, which is fully documented in my Best Buy receipt dated XX/XX/XXXX. 

Despite the return and exchange, my account was billed for both televisions the original {$1600.00} and the new purchase of {$1700.00}. I never retained both TVs, yet I am being held financially responsible for a duplicate charge. 

Over the past several months, I have disputed this charge multiple times directly with Best Buy Credit Services. Each time, they have denied my dispute, claiming no billing error occurred. Their denial letter dated XX/XX/XXXX states that the documentation or facts provided do not support the billing error dispute claim and that a refund slip was not provided. This is unacceptable, as I provided proof of return and exchange via the official Best Buy receipt. 

Best Buy Credit Services has failed to properly investigate this dispute and has continued to include the disputed {$1600.00} in my balance. This is a clear violation of my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ), which protects consumers from being billed for items not retained. 

I have been disputing this for months with no resolution, and I am now seeking CFPB assistance to enforce my rights and hold Best Buy Credit Services accountable for improper billing and failure to resolve a legitimate dispute.
